json.headline: AI/ML Engineer & Lead Software Developer @ Omnic.AI | 3+YOE | MIT Sloan Analytics Challenge Finalist | MS in Computer Science @ Northeastern University | Open to Full-Time Opportunities Starting July 2025
json.topSkills: Software Development • Artificial Intelligence (AI) • Python (Programming Language) • Machine Learning • Amazon Web Services (AWS)
json.about:
As an AI/ML Software Engineer at Omnic.AI, I specialized in prompt engineering and machine learning algorithm development to deliver actionable insights that enhanced player performance in Rocket League. My work played a pivotal role in strategic gameplay analysis and led to our recognition as a Finalist at the MIT Sloan Sports Analytics Conference, underscoring the real-world impact of our AI-driven solutions.

At Northeastern University, I further deepened my expertise as a Research Assistant in Deep Learning Genomics, where I applied state-of-the-art AI techniques to tackle complex biological data challenges. This blend of academic research and industry application reflects my commitment to advancing the field of machine learning and artificial intelligence.

I am passionate about leveraging AI to build innovative, data-driven technologies that address real-world problems. As I actively seek full-time opportunities starting July 2025, I aim to contribute to forward-thinking teams focused on transformative AI solutions.

json.about:
I am a builder & developer who thrives on turning ambitious ideas into production systems. My path started as the Valedictorian at Mumbai University, where I published research in deep learning, wireless communication, and fintech, and carried forward into professional engineering roles across banking, AI, and blockchain.

At IDFC FIRST Bank, I engineered components of core banking systems, optimizing backend workflows and strengthening financial infrastructure at scale. Moving to New York City, I immersed myself in advanced computing at Cornell University, Cornell Tech, where I explore machine learning, optimization, human–computer interaction, and system design, while leading as a teaching assistant and technical program manager guiding teams to ship applied AI products with industry partners.

Most recently at DIMO, I pushed the edge of Web3 and AI by building across domains: smart contracts and backend APIs, decentralized incentive systems, and full-stack AI applications. I developed RoadRacer, the first Coinbase Wallet mini-app on Base; DrivePool, a mobility rewards platform; and DIMO AI, an intelligent assistant that transforms vehicles into smartcars with real-time telemetry, predictive diagnostics, document intelligence, and conversational AI integrated through the Model Context Protocol.

I do not limit myself to a single domain. Whether it is backend engineering, distributed systems, machine learning pipelines, or decentralized applications, I build with the same intensity: shipping products that are scalable, intelligent and designed for real impact.

publications.title: Compact Designs of Circular Microstrip Antennas Employing Modified Ground Plane for Wideband Response
publications.publishedAt: Elsevier's AEU - International Journal of Electronics Communications · Jan 18, 2024
publications.description:
• Ground plane slots reduces patch fundamental mode frequency and Bow-tie shape of the same yields wider bandwidth on thinner substrate, thus achieving wider bandwidth with a smaller antenna volume
• Against the conventional ground plane design, modified ground plane antenna yields 25% bandwidth increment, 0.03 λg  reduction in substrate thickness, 35.12% reduction in the resonance frequency, 66% reduction in the patch area with a broadside peak gain of 5.5

publications.title: Stock Movement Using State-of-the-art Transformer Model
publications.publishedAt: International Conference on Advances in Computer Engineering, Communication Systems and Business Development (ICACECSBD-2023) · Jun 26, 2023
publications.description:
ISBN : 978-93-92105-64-7

• For stock price prediction, a comparison of transformer-based model was proposed with two other models namely ARIMA and LSTM (RNN), on observation transformers were faster than actual RNN-based models such as LSTM as all the input are ingested once
• Evaluated the models through various parameters after training and testing the validated score of Mean Square Error of 0.0004091 (least for transformer model) was obtained for the given Amazon Stocks Time series dataset
• The training and evaluation demonstrated that the proposed model architectures that split time series into patches sufficed for identifying complex patterns in financial time series irregularities. Transformer architecture also showed inflections in stock graphs as observed in the results with precision

publications.title: Wideband and Compact Regular Shape Microstrip Antennas Employing Rectangular Slots Cut Bow-Tie Shape Ground Plane
publications.publishedAt: Progress In Electromagnetics Research B (PIER B), Vol. 100, 155-172, 2023 · Jun 24, 2023
publications.description:
• Wide bandwidth compact rectangular and equilateral triangular microstrip antennas employing slots cut bow-tie shape ground plane profile are proposed. 
• Amongst all the designs, patch employing three rectangular slots cut bow-tie shape ground plane yields optimum results. Using the rectangular patch, against conventional ground plane design, increase in bandwidth by 20%, resonance frequency, substrate thickness, and patch area reduction by 32%, 0.34 λg, and 61.12%, are respectively achieved
• Proposed designs exhibit broadside radiation pattern with broadside gain of above 5 dBi.

Detection
publications.publishedAt: Institution of Electronics and Telecommunication Engineers (IETE-SF) Spark Journal · Apr 3, 2023
publications.description:

-Developed CNN-based DenseNet-121 and ResNet-50 models for detecting glaucoma from extracted spatial data in images. 
- The DenseNet-121 model achieved an average F-measure of 85% and accuracy of 74%, whereas the ResNet-50 model achieved an average F-measure of 83% and accuracy of 72% in distinguishing glaucoma-positive from healthy eyes. 
- Conducted many tests with diverse numbers of epochs, batch sizes, and learning rates to identify the optimum conditions for each model. The DenseNet model, which we trained and evaluated using spatial information, excelled the ResNet model with respect to accuracy
